summaries.74233: Comments confirm the bug is reproducible on latest stable and master channels on macOS, with both `TextInputAction.done` and `TextInputAction.newline` failing to respect the shift-key modifier. A workaround using `RawKeyboardListener` with `TextInputAction.unspecified` was shared in the first comment. The issue remains open with no fix or PR referenced; the only triage activity was a bot note that the `triaged-desktop` label is irrelevant without a `team-desktop` label.
summaries.91240: Comments narrow the issue to Safari on macOS only — Chrome is unaffected and other websites work fine. Investigation with added key-event logging revealed that Safari itself is not delivering `keydown` events to the page for the affected keys (s, d, f, e), so the root cause is a browser-level event suppression, not a Flutter bug. The Flutter team was unable to reproduce on any of their machines and had no further ideas; the issue is effectively stalled with no fix or workaround proposed.
summaries.159246: The crash is confirmed iOS-only (reported on iPhone 12 Pro Max iOS 18.0.1 and iPhone 14 iOS 17.6.1); Android is unaffected. The symbolicated stack trace points to a UIKit accessibility call (`UIFocusRingManagerAccessibility`), and a commenter noted the crash may be unrelated to the webview_flutter_android import that the reporter removed as a workaround. The reporter confirmed that commenting out `import 'package:webview_flutter_android/webview_flutter_android.dart'` in CI/CD stopped the crashes, but team members questioned whether this was actually the cause given the accessibility-focused crash trace. The issue remains open and unresolved, with the team unable to reproduce it in debug mode.
summaries.58498: Comments confirm the issue persists through Flutter 3.29.2 and is primarily reproducible on iOS Safari, though some commenters also see it on Android Chrome. The root cause is identified in comment #34 as a browser security restriction: mobile browsers refuse to open the keyboard unless `focus()` is called in direct response to a user gesture, making automatic keyboard-on-load impossible in web. A workaround of removing `autofocus: true` and calling `requestFocus()` after a delay was shared (#35) and confirmed working on Android but not reliably on iOS Safari. The issue carries a `workaround available` label but remains open and unresolved for iOS Safari specifically.
